findings.finding: JSON-LD structured data is emitted as literal template text instead of valid JSON.
findings.evidence: dist/index.html contains three application/ld+json blocks with raw content {JSON.stringify(webSiteSchema)}, {JSON.stringify(softwareSchema)}, and {JSON.stringify(faqSchema)}.
findings.fix: Render JSON-LD with Astro set:html or precomputed JSON strings; validate dist/index.html after build.

findings.finding: Every generated page uses the homepage canonical URL.
findings.evidence: page-summary.json shows canonical https://contentfinderwp.com/ for index, imprint, privacy-policy, and 404.html.
findings.fix: Make canonical path-aware in Layout.astro; set /imprint/ and /privacy-policy/ canonicals to their own URLs; omit or noindex 404.

findings.finding: FAQPage schema is included on a commercial software site where Google rich-result eligibility is restricted.
findings.evidence: Layout.astro includes faqSchema globally; generated pages contain a FAQPage JSON-LD placeholder. SEO skill rule: FAQPage rich results are restricted to government/health authority sites.
findings.fix: Remove FAQPage JSON-LD from commercial pages; keep visible FAQ content but do not emit FAQPage schema.

findings.finding: Sitemap only lists the homepage and robots.txt has no Sitemap directive.
findings.evidence: dist/sitemap-0.xml contains only https://contentfinderwp.com/; robots checker reports 'No Sitemap directive found in robots.txt'.
findings.fix: Configure Astro sitemap to include intended indexable routes and add Sitemap: https://contentfinderwp.com/sitemap-index.xml to robots.txt.

findings.evidence: llms_txt_checker reports /llms.txt HTTP 404 and llms-full.txt not found.
findings.fix: Add /llms.txt with product summary, key URLs, pricing, trust/safety notes, and docs-like concise facts.

findings.finding: Some AI crawlers are not explicitly managed.
findings.evidence: robots checker reports ChatGPT-User, anthropic-ai, and FacebookBot inherit default rules.
findings.fix: Add explicit user-agent directives for these crawlers according to desired AI indexing/training policy.

findings.finding: OG title is longer than recommended social preview length.
findings.evidence: social_meta.py reports og:title is 70 chars, max 60.
findings.fix: Shorten default OG title, e.g. 'WEUX Content Finder | WordPress content search'.

findings.finding: Internal link graph is thin and legal pages dominate internal anchors.
findings.evidence: internal_links.py crawled 3 pages, found only 8 internal links; top anchors are Imprint and Privacy Policy; homepage parse only lists legal internal links because hash links are not page URLs.
findings.fix: Add meaningful internal links to /imprint/ and /privacy-policy/ only where needed; consider real section URLs only if adding separate pages later. Ensure footer links support key conversion pages.

findings.finding: Local dev response lacks production security headers.
findings.evidence: security_headers.py against localhost reports missing HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, and Permissions-Policy. Local HTTP is not production evidence.
findings.fix: Configure Cloudflare Pages headers via _headers or middleware for production; verify on deployed HTTPS URL.

findings.finding: Matomo adds a third-party script that should be monitored for performance and privacy impact.
findings.evidence: Layout.astro loads //analyse.marcweidemueller.de/matomo.js globally. PageSpeed was rate-limited, so no CWV metric was measured.
findings.fix: Keep Matomo async, verify no cookies if desired, and re-run PageSpeed/Lighthouse on production.
